title: Lesson 23 — The Scholars

words.definition: having or showing great knowledge; learned
words.example: An erudite lecture packed with apt references.

words.definition: having keen mental discernment and good judgment; wise
words.example: A sagacious advisor whose counsel was always sound.
An astute investor who spotted the trend early.

words.definition: having or showing good judgment; perceptive
words.example: A discerning critic with famously good taste.
A perspicacious reader who caught the hidden clue.

words.definition: a person overly concerned with minor details, rules, or displaying learning
words.example: A pedant who corrected everyone's grammar at dinner.

words.definition: intended to teach, often in a heavy-handed or moralizing way
words.example: A didactic novel that lectured its readers on every page.

words.definition: a person of great learning or exceptional ability
words.example: A math savant who solved the puzzle in seconds.

words.definition: the ability to make good judgments and quick decisions
words.example: Her business acumen quietly saved the firm.
The old sage offered only a few quiet words of advice.

words.definition: intellectual rather than emotional or physical
words.example: A cerebral film that rewards close attention.

words.definition: involving or relating to serious academic study
words.example: A scholarly edition with hundreds of footnotes.

story: The seminar drew the department's sharpest minds. Professor Vance was [[erudite|erudite]] and [[sagacious|sagacious]], his questions [[astute|astute]] and [[discerning|discerning]]; the visiting fellow was even more [[perspicacious|perspicacious]]. One [[pedant|pedant]] in the corner kept interrupting to correct citations, and a [[didactic|didactic]] guest lectured the room as if to freshmen. But it was the quiet [[savant|savant]] by the window — all [[acumen|acumen]] and no show — whom the others treated as a [[sage|sage]]. Her comments were [[cerebral|cerebral]] yet clear, more illuminating than any [[scholarly|scholarly]] footnote.
